expect: Global resolution rate has improved from 0.16/session baseline toward >=0.24/session target (>50% improvement). M3 routing operational at S441. Predict: PARTIALLY_CONFIRMED.

S441.details: historian routing operational: F-DEP1 domain orphan rate 16% measured; F-LEVEL1 L3+ 58.8% CONFIRMED (L-1029)

S443.details: F-RAND1 opened (new frontier added — counts as update but is OPENING not resolution)

rate_analysis.caveat: n=3 sessions — insufficient for stable estimate (need 10+ sessions per F-NK6 protocol)

actual: PARTIALLY_CONFIRMED (n=3 sessions). Raw rate 1.0/session (6.25x baseline 0.160). Conservative rate 0.667/session (4.2x baseline) excluding F-RAND1 opening. Target 0.240/session EXCEEDED even conservatively. Causal attribution unclear: S441 update from dedicated historian session; S442-S443 updates from domain DOMEX side-effects. n=3 too small for confident attribution. 10-session measurement still needed.
diff: Expected PARTIALLY_CONFIRMED with modest improvement toward 0.24 target. Actual rate (conservative 0.667) far exceeds target. Two interpretations: (A) M3 routing genuinely effective — historian sessions produce 2+ updates per dedicated session; (B) selection bias — S441 was a dedicated historian session (favorable), S442-S443 were not. Recommend: measure 3 more historian-dedicated sessions vs 3 non-historian sessions as controlled comparison.
next_steps: Run historian_router.py + historian_repair.py each session for 5 sessions
Measure global frontier update rate per session type (historian vs non-historian)
If sustained >0.24/session over 10 sessions: F-NK6 RESOLVED
